article.description: — Anthony Black scored 26 points, Desmond Bane added 25 and the Orlando Magic beat Milwaukee 118-99 on Monday night to snap the Bucks' season-long three-game win streak.
article.headline: Black scores 26, Bane adds 25 for Magic in 118-99 win over Bucks

images.caption: Milwaukee Bucks vs. Orlando Magic: Game Highlights
images.name: Milwaukee Bucks vs. Orlando Magic: Game Highlights

article.linkText: Black scores 26, Bane adds 25 for Magic in 118-99 win over Bucks
article.story: ORLANDO, Fla. -- — Anthony Black scored 26 points, Desmond Bane added 25 and the Orlando Magic beat Milwaukee 118-99 on Monday night to snap the Bucks' season-long three-game win streak. Magic forward Franz Wagner played 17 minutes after missing 23 of the last 25 games due to a high ankle sprain. Wagner came off the bench for the first time in his career and finished with 14 points and five rebounds. Kevin Porter Jr. led Milwaukee with 28 points and seven assists, but the Bucks committed 20 turnovers and gave up 67 points in the second half. that ended their season-long three-game win streak. Milwaukee All-Star Giannis Antetokounmpo (calf) missed his 15th straight game. Mo Wagner hit two 3-pointers, and brother Franz and Jase Richardson hit one each in a 14-2 burst late in the third quarter that gave the Magic a 15-point lead. Black took over in the fourth quarter, scoring Orlando's first three baskets and later adding a 3-pointer that stretched the lead to 20. The Magic made 4 of 21 (19%) from 3-point range in the first half and 10 of 21 (48%) in the second half. Veteran guard Cam Thomas, signed by Milwaukee as a free agent on Sunday, scored four points in 13 minutes. Jevon Carter played the final four minutes in his Magic debut after signing on Friday. Up next The Bucks: Play the Magic again Wednesday night in Orlando.

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs handles the ball during the game against the Detroit Pistons on March 5, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.

images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.

images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
